project.name: spring-rest-sakila
project.description: A Spring Boot REST API service that provides full CRUD operations and complex query support for the MySQL Sakila sample database, which models a DVD rental store company. The service exposes endpoints for managing films, actors, customers, rentals, payments, staff, stores, and locations, with features including role-based access control, JWT authentication, and HAL-based hypermedia responses.
project.architecture_summary: The application follows a layered architecture with clear separation of concerns: a REST controller layer handling HTTP endpoints, a service layer containing business logic, a repository layer providing data access with both standard Spring Data JPA and custom implementations, mapper components for DTOs, and a comprehensive configuration layer. The code is organized into feature modules (actors, films, customers, rentals, payments, staff, stores, locations, authentication, reporting) with each module containing its own controllers, services, repositories, DTOs, and entities. Cross-cutting concerns are handled through centralized configuration classes and filters (security, authentication, logging, exception handling).
